reviews.review_msg: Casey read off the slides, the information came directly from the TB, and the lectures were three hours. Additionally, the midterm was worth 45% and the final 55% of your grade, which made it challenging to do well in this class if you recieved a bad grade on one or the other.

Casey knew a lot about the course content and I thought she was very nice. Her lectures were interesting to me, though I purposely avoided reading the textbook before lectures because the they were just the chapters with less detail and then I'd read it after class. There were in-class activities, but one non-exam component would have been helpful.
55% of your grade is final exam, 45% is midterm. Half the content from lectures don't match what's tested and were each over 100 questions.

Dr. Kirkpatrick is a very friendly professor. She gives us exam information, such as the types of questions and materials to focus on, in advance and creates fun activities to help us understand the concepts. She also gave me information about the exam schedule when I emailed her about my absence due to a conference (if that info is given).

Every single week we had to read two chapters which TOOK HOURS! I learned ZILCH in lectures. The teacher regurgetated the material she had already given us, and if it was possible to die of boredom, my soul has already left this mortal plane two months past!

The exams were BRUTAL. More than a 100 questions on ancient monkeys! WHY?!
